steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Set up the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
A right triangle has one leg of 39.6 mm and a hypotenuse of 180.4 mm. Using the Pythagorean theorem, where $a = 39.6$, $c = 180.4$, and $b$ is the unknown leg:
$$39.6^2 + b^2 = 180.4^2$$

steps.title: Calculate and solve for the other leg
steps.content:
Compute both squares:
$$1568.16 + b^2 = 32544.16$$
Subtract 1568.16 from both sides:
$$b^2 = 30976$$
Take the positive square root:
$$b = \sqrt{30976} = 176$$
C is correct.
